meta.total_spans_note: Corrected from 48 (which included working-directory-only changes from partial files not committed to branch). Sum of per_file spans for 16 files_with_spans = 38.
meta.file_count_note: files_on_branch (17) vs files_with_spans (16): monthly-summary-prompt.js is on the branch with unused OTel imports but 0 spans, accounting for the difference. files_failed (3) are summary-graph.js, sensitive-filter.js, and journal-manager.js — reported as 'partial' in the PR summary but have NO changes on the orbweaver branch (instrumentation never committed). rubric-scores.json uses files_instrumented_on_branch (16) which equals files_with_spans.

NDS-001.evidence: node --check would pass on all files. The tracer ReferenceError in summary-graph.js and sensitive-filter.js occurs at function invocation time, not at module parse/syntax level. No .ts files added to the JS project.
NDS-001.note: NDS-002 (test suite) provides the stronger signal for the broken tracer references.

NDS-002.evidence: 32 test failures, all ReferenceError: tracer is not defined. Caused by summary-graph.js (21 failures across 3 test files + 4 downstream) and sensitive-filter.js (11 failures). Function-level fallback added tracer.startActiveSpan() calls without adding tracer import/initialization.
NDS-002.note: These changes exist only in the working directory during the run — they were never committed to the branch. The tests failed at end-of-run when the working directory contained the broken code.

API-001.evidence: All 17 modified source files import only from '@opentelemetry/api'. No imports from @opentelemetry/sdk-*, @opentelemetry/exporter-*, or @opentelemetry/instrumentation-*. grep for non-API imports returned empty.

API-002.evidence: @opentelemetry/api is in peerDependencies (^1.9.0). commit-story-v2 is distributed as an npm package (library), so peerDependencies is the correct location per OTel specification.
API-002.note: The @opentelemetry/api entry was already present before the orbweaver run (from manual setup). The agent preserved it correctly.

API-003.evidence: No vendor-specific SDK packages (dd-trace, @newrelic/telemetry-sdk, @splunk/otel) in package.json. Agent added @traceloop/instrumentation-langchain and @traceloop/instrumentation-mcp as optional peerDependencies — these are community auto-instrumentation libraries, not vendor SDKs.
API-003.note: @traceloop packages use Traceloop-specific attributes (traceloop.span.kind, traceloop.entity.*) internally, which could be a concern for vendor neutrality. However, as optional peerDependencies, they don't force installation.

API-004.evidence: No @opentelemetry/sdk-* or @opentelemetry/instrumentation-* imports in any source file. All OTel imports resolve to @opentelemetry/api only. SDK setup file (src/instrumentation.js) would be exempt but was not modified.

NDS-006.evidence: All agent-added imports use ESM syntax (import ... from ...). No require() or module.exports detected. Project is ESM (type: module in package.json).

CDQ-008.evidence: All 17 files with tracer initialization use trace.getTracer('unknown_service'). Single naming pattern across entire codebase — consistent.
CDQ-008.note: While consistent (CDQ-008 pass), the name 'unknown_service' is wrong — it should be the library name 'commit-story' (CDQ-002 fail). CDQ-008 evaluates consistency, not correctness.

NDS-003.evidence: Diff shows only instrumentation additions: import, tracer, startActiveSpan wrapper, setAttribute calls, recordException, setStatus, span.end. No non-instrumentation lines changed.

NDS-004.evidence: All 8 exported functions preserve original signatures. collectChatMessages wrapped in span callback but signature unchanged.

NDS-005.evidence: parseJSONLFile try/catch (skip malformed JSON, continue) preserved. collectChatMessages error handling wrapped in span but original behavior maintained.

NDS-003.evidence: In runSummarize(), the original `catch { // Doesn't exist, proceed }` block (access() check for existing summary) was changed to `catch (accessErr) { span.recordException(accessErr); span.setStatus({ code: SpanStatusCode.ERROR }); }`. This records a file-not-found error as a span exception for what is NORMAL control flow (file not existing = generate new summary). Semantic behavioral change.

NDS-005.evidence: The original `catch { // Doesn't exist, proceed }` intentionally swallowed the fs access error (expected control flow). The instrumented version records this as an exception and sets ERROR status. setStatus is a one-way latch in OTel — once set to ERROR, it cannot be changed back to OK, so the span is permanently marked ERROR even if the function succeeds.

COV-001.evidence: handleSummarize entry point covered via summarize.run_daily/weekly/monthly spans.

COV-002.evidence: No direct outbound calls — delegates to summary-manager.

COV-003.evidence: Error recording present on all 3 spans.

COV-005.evidence: Uses commit_story.summarize.* attributes. Ad-hoc attributes: commit_story.summarize.input_count, commit_story.summarize.force, commit_story.summarize.generated_count, commit_story.summarize.failed_count — not in registry.

SCH-002.evidence: 4 ad-hoc attribute keys not in registry: commit_story.summarize.input_count, commit_story.summarize.force, commit_story.summarize.generated_count, commit_story.summarize.failed_count. These follow the namespace convention but were not pre-registered.

SCH-003.evidence: Attribute values are appropriate types (integers for counts, boolean for force).

SCH-004.evidence: No redundant attributes — ad-hoc keys are unique domain concepts.

CDQ-003.evidence: In runSummarize(), recordException + setStatus(ERROR) used on a non-error control flow path — file-not-found is the expected condition for generating a new summary. Misuse of error recording pattern.

CDQ-006.evidence: No expensive computation in setAttribute calls.

NDS-003.evidence: In all 3 generateAndSave* functions, the `access()` catch blocks (which check if file exists) changed from `catch { // Doesn't exist, proceed }` to `catch (error) { span.recordException(error); span.setStatus({ code: SpanStatusCode.ERROR }); }`. ENOENT is the EXPECTED happy path (file doesn't exist = generate new). Recording it as exception + ERROR pollutes telemetry.

NDS-005.evidence: Original `access()` catch blocks used silent `catch {}` because file-not-found is expected control flow. Instrumented version records these as exceptions and sets ERROR status, changing error handling semantics. Span shows ERROR even when function succeeds.

src/generators/summary-graph.js.branch_status: NO CHANGES ON BRANCH — instrumentation never committed
src/generators/summary-graph.js.failure_reason: Function-level fallback produced instrumentation but missing tracer import. Changes were applied to working directory during run but never committed to the branch. 21 test failures caused.
src/generators/summary-graph.js.nds_evaluation: NDS-002 FAIL (21 test failures). Would also fail NDS-003 if committed (unknown — changes not on branch to evaluate).

src/integrators/filters/sensitive-filter.branch_status: NO CHANGES ON BRANCH — instrumentation never committed
src/integrators/filters/sensitive-filter.failure_reason: Function-level fallback instrumented redactMessages and applySensitiveFilter but missing tracer import. redactSensitiveData blocked by NDS-003 (12 regex pattern violations). 11 test failures caused.
src/integrators/filters/sensitive-filter.nds_evaluation: NDS-002 FAIL (11 test failures). redactSensitiveData would fail NDS-003 (regex pattern mangling).

src/managers/journal-manager.js.branch_status: NO CHANGES ON BRANCH — 0 spans means no useful instrumentation delivered
src/managers/journal-manager.js.failure_reason: formatJournalEntry processed correctly (0 spans — right decision). saveJournalEntry and discoverReflections blocked by NDS-003 (business logic modifications). Net result: 0 spans, file effectively uninstrumented.
src/managers/journal-manager.js.nds_evaluation: No test failures. NDS-003 correctly blocked non-instrumentation changes on saveJournalEntry and discoverReflections.

src/generators/prompts/guidelines/access.reason: Pure data export — string constant (prompt template). No I/O, no async, no entry point.

src/generators/prompts/guidelines/index..reason: Re-exports and getAllGuidelines() (pure function returning concatenated strings). No I/O.

src/generators/prompts/sections/daily-su.reason: Exports dailySummaryPrompt() — pure function returning template string.

src/generators/prompts/sections/monthly-.reason: Exports monthlySummaryPrompt() — pure template function. NOTE: Agent added unused OTel import + tracer init despite 0 spans. Minor waste (RST concern) but not harmful.

gates.note: NDS-002 fails at the run level (32 test failures). However, the failing code was never committed to the branch — the branch contains only passing instrumentation. The test failures occurred in the working directory during the run.

NDS.note: NDS-003 fail: summarize.js, summary-manager.js (ENOENT catch blocks changed to record errors). NDS-005 fail: summarize.js, summary-manager.js, summary-detector.js (expected-condition catch blocks now record exceptions). NDS-004 pass all 16 files. git-collector.js NDS-005 verified pass.

COV.note: Key gaps: index.js missing root span (COV-001 fail), claude-collector.js missing findJSONLFiles/parseJSONLFile spans (COV-002 fail), ad-hoc attributes in 3 files (COV-005 fail).

RST.note: RST-001 fail: token-filter.js (truncateDiff/truncateMessages are pure sync functions with spans). 2 borderline on RST-004 (unexported I/O helpers in MCP tools).

API.note: Perfect API compliance. All imports from @opentelemetry/api only.

SCH.note: SCH-001 fails: 4 files use non-commit_story.* span names. SCH-002 fails: 3 files use ad-hoc attributes not in registry (broken schema evolution).

CDQ.note: CDQ-002 fails all 16 files (trace.getTracer('unknown_service')). CDQ-003 fails 3 files (summarize.js, summary-manager.js, summary-detector.js — expected ENOENT recorded as errors). CDQ-006 fails 2 files (toISOString without isRecording guard).

key_findings: CDQ-002: All 16 files use trace.getTracer('unknown_service') instead of 'commit-story'. This is a systemic agent bug — the library name should match the package name.
SCH-001: 8 of 37 span names deviate from commit_story.* prefix (context.*, mcp.*, summary.*). The agent is inconsistent across file boundaries — schema evolution would have helped enforce naming.
SCH-002: 11 ad-hoc attributes not in registry. These would have been registered via schema evolution if it had worked (orb issue #1). Follow commit_story.* namespace but are not pre-registered.
NDS-002: 32 test failures from function-level fallback missing tracer imports. Changes were never committed to branch — the branch is clean.
COV-001: index.js missing root span on main(). The trace has no top-level CLI operation span.

NDS-003/NDS-005: summarize.js, summary-manager.js, summary-detector.js have expected-condition catch blocks (ENOENT file-not-found) changed to record exceptions + set ERROR status. This is a semantic behavioral change — the spans report ERROR for normal control flow. OTel's setStatus is a one-way latch, so the span is permanently marked ERROR even when the function succeeds.
RST-001: token-filter.js has spans on truncateDiff() and truncateMessages() — pure synchronous data transformation functions that should not be instrumented.
Zero-span decisions: All 10 correct. monthly-summary-prompt.js had unused imports added (minor waste).
Schema evolution broken: All 29 files received identical base schema. 0 extensions were registered across the entire run.
